# Questions in Livestream 39

Live Stream #39: Master of the Sock Puppets

Time links open the YouTube video at the relevant timestamp. The links use HTML anchors so GitHub can render them with `target="_blank"`.

| Time | Question | Short answer / answer direction |
|---:|---|---|
| <a href="https://youtu.be/qi7VuxyqWr8?t=214" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:34</a> | Who is his favorite superhero? | The Tick. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/qi7VuxyqWr8?t=285" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">4:45</a> | What is his opinion of D. A. Carson and Carson's defense of tradition? | Falk called Carson a brilliant but prickly scholar and said appeals to extra-biblical tradition have to be handled very cautiously. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/qi7VuxyqWr8?t=572" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">9:32</a> | What did Jesus mean by vain repetition and babbling in prayer? | Falk said the warning targets meaningless, mechanical prayer rather than every repeated prayer formula. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/qi7VuxyqWr8?t=735" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">12:15</a> | Were the ten plagues meant as insults against Egyptian gods? | He rejected the usual full-plague theory and said only the final plague has a clearer claim to being judgment on the "gods of Egypt." |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/qi7VuxyqWr8?t=937" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">15:37</a> | Is there evidence for the events in Esther, Ezra, and Nehemiah? | He said the question was too broad for an off-the-cuff answer and did not want to bluff certainty. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/qi7VuxyqWr8?t=1068" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">17:48</a> | Is Revelation mainly about Nero, or is Nero only a type within a future-oriented prophecy? | Falk took Nero as an antichrist type used within a still future-facing prophecy, not as the whole fulfillment. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/qi7VuxyqWr8?t=1352" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">22:32</a> | Where did the tribe of Dan come from, and what happened to it? | He treated Dan as an Israelite tribe that later fragmented and was dispersed like the other northern tribes under Assyria. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/qi7VuxyqWr8?t=1669" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">27:49</a> | What were scarabs, and why are they found everywhere? | He described them as protective amulets and exchange objects that spread widely because people carried, traded, and deposited them so often. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/qi7VuxyqWr8?t=1964" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">32:44</a> | Could Atlantis and the flood both derive from Egyptian source traditions? | A flood tradition has clearer ancient parallels, but Atlantis remains unconfirmed; he allowed only the possibility of a now-lost Egyptian source. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/qi7VuxyqWr8?t=2282" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">38:02</a> | Is El Shaddai best understood as a creator-god title used before the name Yahweh? | He thought that was a strong possibility and linked it to how God may have been known to Abraham. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/qi7VuxyqWr8?t=2413" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">40:13</a> | When did people figure out that thought comes from the brain rather than the heart? | He placed the major shift in early modern and especially nineteenth-century medicine, while noting ancient and biblical language still uses "heart" for thought. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/qi7VuxyqWr8?t=2723" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">45:23</a> | Why is "Rameses" in Genesis a retrojection while "Rameses" in Exodus is not? | Because Genesis uses later labels for an earlier setting, while Exodus names a route whose other place names fit the Ramesside period itself. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/qi7VuxyqWr8?t=3149" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">52:29</a> | Did he play Assassin's Creed Origins, and how accurate was its portrayal of Ptolemaic Egypt and the Medjay? | He said the villages looked impressively Egyptian, but the game took artistic liberties with buildings, weapons, and details that cannot be checked. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/qi7VuxyqWr8?t=3446" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">57:26</a> | What does he think about recent Hatshepsut statue fragments and the cache of 18,000 inscribed pottery shards? | The statue fragments did not excite him much, but the inscribed pottery cache did because ostraca can preserve major social and historical data. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/qi7VuxyqWr8?t=3717" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:01:57</a> | How was Bronze Age warfare actually conducted? | He said it changed over time, moving from infantry with maces and spears to mixed infantry-and-chariot warfare, with chariots mainly used as mobile archery platforms. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/qi7VuxyqWr8?t=4067" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:07:47</a> | Does Psalm 136:15 lie about Pharaoh's fate in the sea? | No. Falk argued the Hebrew is better understood as God brushing Pharaoh and his army aside, not explicitly saying Pharaoh drowned there. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/qi7VuxyqWr8?t=4422" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:13:42</a> | Is Psalm 104 a polemical adaptation of the Hymn to the Aten? | Yes, he thought the dependence and polemical reuse are very likely. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/qi7VuxyqWr8?t=4709" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:18:29</a> | When the Jerusalem temple still stood, were Israelites required to worship Yahweh only there? | Yes for official sacrifice, though local Levites, Scripture study, prayer, and later synagogue practice supported worship and instruction away from Jerusalem. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/qi7VuxyqWr8?t=5045" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:24:05</a> | What is his opinion of Robert E. Howard's Solomon Kane, Conan, and Kull stories? | He liked the old Conan films but had not read Howard's work, so he did not offer a literary opinion. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/qi7VuxyqWr8?t=5213" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:26:53</a> | What does he think about dating the judges, and did many of them overlap? | He said Judges should be read in parallel rather than as a simple sequence, since the years otherwise stretch unrealistically long. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/qi7VuxyqWr8?t=5469" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:31:09</a> | What does he think of Yu-Gi-Oh as an Egyptologist and anime fan? | He had not watched it yet, though he likes anime and was willing to consider it. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/qi7VuxyqWr8?t=5555" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:32:35</a> | Would he consider making a video about newly published Aramaic papyri from Elephantine? | Yes, if he could access the publication and found something interesting to report. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/qi7VuxyqWr8?t=5651" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:34:11</a> | If the Ten Commandments were written in Hebrew, what script was used? | Falk said they were probably written in an early alphabetic Semitic script rather than hieroglyphs. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/qi7VuxyqWr8?t=5978" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:39:38</a> | What elements would make a fantasy RPG setting feel Middle Bronze Age rather than medieval? | He suggested removing steel, heavy armor, and most chariots; using foot soldiers, stone arrowheads, small fortified cities, and Bronze Age foods and settlement patterns. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/qi7VuxyqWr8?t=6349" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:45:49</a> | Who were Amun and Re, and why were they sometimes combined as Amun-Re? | He described Amun as Thebes' hidden god and Re as the royal sun god, with their merger elevating Theban theology into the royal cult. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/qi7VuxyqWr8?t=6737" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:52:17</a> | Does Deuteronomy 11:4 imply Egypt's whole army was destroyed for forty years? | No. Falk said the passage specifically focuses on horses and chariots, so Egypt lost its chariot arm rather than every military force. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/qi7VuxyqWr8?t=7137" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:58:57</a> | Are at least some lesser deities shades, or are they other kinds of spiritual beings? | He said ancient terms for "god" cover a wide range of spiritual beings, from great gods to lesser spirits, and Scripture also reflects a broad spiritual hierarchy. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/qi7VuxyqWr8?t=7414" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:03:34</a> | How would people commonly have known Yahweh in Abraham's time? | Falk said they likely knew him by El Shaddai rather than the name Yahweh, which Moses brings back from Midian. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/qi7VuxyqWr8?t=7512" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:05:12</a> | Since the Ninevites were polytheists, what would they have understood when Jonah told them to repent? | He said they could understand divine anger and repentance from violence without necessarily becoming full monotheists. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/qi7VuxyqWr8?t=8070" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:14:30</a> | Did Egyptians discover honey and beeswax, and is there a connection to the land of milk and honey? | Egyptians practiced beekeeping and used honey and wax, but "milk and honey" refers more directly to goat milk and wild honey rather than Egyptian discovery. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/qi7VuxyqWr8?t=8304" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:18:24</a> | Does he think Job was a historical person? | Yes, though he thinks Job's story likely has complex reception history and may have moved from an Aramaic setting into Hebrew. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/qi7VuxyqWr8?t=8472" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:21:12</a> | Does he prefer a maximalist or minimalist approach to the resurrection? | Maximalist: he affirmed the historical bodily resurrection of Jesus and the future resurrection of the dead. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/qi7VuxyqWr8?t=8599" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:23:19</a> | Did Egypt's holidays balance out the nine-and-a-half-day work week? | Not really. Some festival days were off, but Falk said ancient Egyptian work life remained hard and the records do not show enough holidays to offset it. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/qi7VuxyqWr8?t=8712" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:25:12</a> | Were Egyptian oracle statues tricking people by using cords, or did they serve another purpose? | Falk said people generally were not fooled; the moving statue enacted a social and religious contract in which the priest spoke as the god's proxy. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/qi7VuxyqWr8?t=8962" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:29:22</a> | Are his scarabs authentic or reproductions? | They are reproductions, both because exporting Egyptian antiquities is illegal and because reproductions still have practical teaching or photography uses. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/qi7VuxyqWr8?t=9265" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:34:25</a> | Why did Elisha call bears to maul the young boys in 2 Kings 2:23, and how old were they? | Falk said they were likely young unmarried men, not small children, and were threatening or goading Elisha toward illicit Bethel worship. |
